name: Hash Fundamentals — HashMap Internals, O(1) Ops

metadata.career_relevance: HashMap is the most-used data structure in Java interviews. Two Sum (HashMap lookup) is the #1 Zerodha/Razorpay warm-up. Subarray Sum = K uses prefix+HashMap — appears in 30% of medium-level problems. Knowing load factor and O(1) vs O(n) worst-case signals senior-level understanding.

hook.what_you_will_build: Two Sum, frequency counting, subarray sum, consecutive sequence — all HashMap patterns.

key_takeaways: HashMap: O(1) avg put/get/containsKey. O(n) worst (all collisions).
ALWAYS use getOrDefault or containsKey before get — never assume key exists
prefixCount.put(0,1): handles subarrays starting at index 0
TreeMap: O(log n), sorted. LinkedHashMap: O(1), insertion order.
int[26] vs HashMap: use int[26] for lowercase alpha — O(1) space
Pre-size HashMap: new HashMap<>(n*4/3+1) to avoid resizing
reflection.next_topic_preview: Next: HashMap Patterns — groupAnagrams, frequency analysis, sliding window with map.
